content_of_post_to_comment: Scapegoats get extraordinarily good at self reliance. Not only do we do everything on our own, we have to actively break the bond between us and our family in order to reach peace. Then we have to stay strong enough to not end up in other abusive situations. We walk along very often. We stay single often because our conditioning has taught us to fall for people who also hurt us. We learn to rely on ourselves only. We are strong and tired. Hugs.
comment: Self-reliance born from scapegoating seems adaptive, but I wonder if the harder edge is that the same skill flattens signal detection—treating all dependence as risk, so even healthy bonds feel unsafe.
